title: Service Rental Car (SRC) / 3rd Party Rental Program / Alternative Transportation / Rideshare
raw_content: Hyundai Motor America encourages its dealers to participate in Service Rental Cars (SRC), 3rd Party Rentals and Rideshares to help maintain customer satisfaction by providing transportation while a vehicle is in service. These options are not considered part of the New Vehicle Limited Warranty (NVLW). For eligible repairs under NVLW or certain goodwill situations, Hyundai Motor America may reimburse dealers for SRC, 3rd Party Rental or Rideshare use, subject to the criteria, documentation, limits, and claim-processing requirements in this section. The section also defines what is not covered, required attachments, timing rules, and Rental Reason Code requirements R4, R5, R6, R8, and R9.
dealer_actions: Dealers are required to complete warranty repairs in a timely manner.
Backordered parts should be placed on a daily parts order or SOP order.
For repairs involving parts on HMA backorder, both parts on backorder screen printout(s) and part arrival record(s) must be attached to the Repair Order.
All SRC, 3rd Party Rental and Rideshare claims must be submitted only if there is a corresponding paid warranty claim, campaign claim, or certain customer pay condition.
If the allowance for ordered parts is exceeded, supporting documentation must be attached to the claim for review.

If incorrect information was entered or there is no corresponding claim, such as a buyback situation, the dealer must contact the WCC through the Warranty Support Portal or by phone for immediate processing.
For R9 Buyback situations, the Service Manager must sign the Repair Order and notate authorizing the rental.
eligibility_conditions: Repair is being performed under the New Vehicle Limited Warranty (NVLW) or certain goodwill conditions.
Repair cannot be completed in the same day and the vehicle is unsafe, inoperative, or requires emergency repairs.
Repair cannot be completed in the same day and non-emergency repairs require the vehicle to be kept overnight.
Parts are on HMA backorder and the vehicle is unsafe, inoperative, or requires emergency repairs.
Parts are on HMA backorder and non-emergency repairs require the vehicle to be kept overnight.
Vehicle has multiple warranty issues where total time to complete repairs requires more than one day.
Vehicle has extensive diagnostic time where total time to complete diagnosis and repairs requires more than one day.
Alternative transportation is provided at the request of HMA’s AMM, FSE, or CA department for technical inspection.
A No Trouble Found (NTF) claim is submitted when the dealer cannot duplicate the customer complaint.
A customer later requests an SRC, 3rd Party Rental, or Rideshare after initially declining or not being offered one, but otherwise meets program criteria.
Rental provided due to HMA backorder and repair issue affects drivability/safety (R4).
Rental provided because repair affects drivability/safety for more than one day, or repair falls over a weekend/holiday (R5).
Rental provided because repair requires extensive diagnosis (R6).
Rental provided due to an open recall without a remedy (R8).
Rental provided for a buyback vehicle as part of customer satisfaction (R9).
coverage_and_reimbursement: Hyundai Motor America reimburses the dealer based on a three-tier daily reimbursement rate for eligible NVLW repairs.
A specific defined daily reimbursement rate applies to 3rd Party Rentals and Rideshares.
Reimbursement may be provided for customer satisfaction purposes when eligibility criteria are met.
HMA can reimburse a maximum of two business days of rental for No Trouble Found (NTF) claims.
The dealer is eligible for reimbursement for rideshare services provided on behalf of the customer.
Ridesharing trips may be to and from the dealership or other customer-requested trips during repairs, subject to the daily maximum.
not_covered: SRC, 3rd Party Rental and Rideshare options are not considered part of the New Vehicle Limited Warranty (NVLW).
Dealer provided shuttle service is not eligible for reimbursement.
Active SRC units cannot be used as a shuttle.

Costs for transportation provided outside warrantable conditions, certain goodwill conditions, certain customer pay conditions, complimentary maintenance, or other program criteria are not reimbursed.
Transportation for vehicles outside warrantable or certain goodwill conditions or otherwise not meeting reimbursement criteria is not covered.
Rental kept beyond completion of warranty repairs is not covered.
Charges for continued rental use after repair completion and customer notification are not covered.
Over-the-counter and retail parts purchases are not eligible for rental coverage due to parts issues or delay.
Dealer scheduling/time issues or servicing delays are not valid reasons for extended rental coverage.
Extended Service Contracts with rental coverage must be used and documented first before HMA goodwill rental is considered.
payment_limitations: Maximum of two rental days (2 business days, Monday to Friday) for No Trouble Found (NTF) claims.
Hyundai SRC pays a maximum of one daily reimbursement rate per day.
Allowance for ordered parts is up to two rental days (2 business days, Monday to Friday) after the part arrives for the dealer to complete the repair.
Rideshare reimbursement is limited to the published daily rate per day.
Ridesharing trips are limited to the maximum daily reimbursement amount.
documents_required: Parts on backorder screen printout(s) and part arrival record(s) attached to the Repair Order when applicable.
TSD Vehicle Use Agreement signed by the customer for SRC claims.
Supporting documents to justify rental days submitted.
Rental agreement or final invoice for 3rd Party Rentals showing RO number, customer name, days, rental model, rental VIN, daily rate, and total amount.
Rental justification form may be required for rentals over 3 days.
Receipts for each ride attached to the Repair Order and maintained with the file copy.
Copy of parts availability screen for R4.
Copy of parts order for R5.
comment_requirements: For R4, additional comments must be entered on the claim including part number and parts order number.
For R5, additional comments must be entered on the claim including which part(s) were SOP.
For R5, detail why the customer was provided rental over holiday/weekend rather than scheduled during a normal work week.
For R6, additional comments must be entered on the claim including Techline case #, FSE, or FAST involvement.
For R8, additional comments must be entered on the claim including remedy-not-available recall information and related part order information if applicable.
For R9, additional comments must be entered on the claim.
For R9, if a CA case exists, the Case # must be included in tech notes with the claim.
RO number and Causal Part must be valid for claim matching.
timing_rules: Unless otherwise required by applicable state law, SRC, 3rd Party Rental, and Ride Share claims should be submitted at the same time as the corresponding repair claim.
Claim submission or resubmission over 30 days is subject to denial.
Claims with incorrect or invalid Repair Claim information may go into hold status for up to 7 days.
If after 7 days the system does not find the corresponding repair claim, the claim will be returned.
claim_processing_risks: Dealers may be denied or charged back reimbursement if delays result from inadequate dealer scheduling.
Dealers may be denied or charged back reimbursement if they failed to access available TSB, HTSS, or other HMA technical resources leading to repair delays.
If the dealer failed to place an expedited parts order, the rental is subject to chargeback.
Claim submission or resubmission over 30 days is subject to denial.
Claims with incorrect or invalid Repair Claim information may be returned if a corresponding repair claim is not found after 7 days.
Chargeback of a warranty claim results in subsequent chargeback of associated SRC / Rental / Rideshare claims.

title: Magnuson–Moss Act
raw_content: This section explains how the Magnuson–Moss Warranty Act regulates written warranties, distinguishes Full and Limited Warranties, and describes Hyundai’s vehicle warranty as a limited warranty. It also outlines dealer obligations to make warranty text available before sale, display and provide the Owner’s Handbook, and convey Hyundai parts and accessories warranty information to purchasers and prospective purchasers.
dealer_actions: The Owner’s Handbook must be displayed prominently in the dealer’s new vehicle showroom.
Dealers should point out the warranty statement in the Owner’s Handbook to the customer at the time of purchase.
Dealers must provide the Owner’s Handbook to the customer at the time of vehicle delivery.
Warranty coverage information must be available for review by prospective consumers.
Dealers are obligated to convey Hyundai Motor America supplied parts and accessories warranty coverage to purchasers and prospective purchasers.
All purchasers or prospective purchasers of Hyundai parts and/or accessories must be provided with a copy of the Hyundai Service Parts and Accessories Limited Warranty.
coverage_and_reimbursement: Hyundai’s vehicle warranty is a limited warranty that complies with the Magnuson–Moss Act.
Hyundai Motor America provides a warranty to retail customers on Hyundai Motor America supplied parts and accessories sold to them.
not_covered: The Act does not require a supplier to give a warranty.
The Act does not stipulate that any specific coverage must be provided.
payment_limitations: Hyundai’s limited warranty limits the duration of implied warranties to the duration of the expressed warranty.
documents_required: Hyundai Service Parts and Accessories Limited Warranty
timing_rules: After December 31, 1976, retailers of customer goods costing more than $5.00 are required to make the written warranty text available prior to sale.

title: Warranty Jurisdiction
raw_content: This section defines where Hyundai’s warranties apply by vehicle specification, distribution channel, and geography. It also states that vehicles built to non-U.S. specifications and operated outside the U.S. are entitled to warranty service under the warranty applicable to that other country. It further includes force-majeure liability exclusions and states that Hyundai Motor America reserves final judgment in warranty matters and may issue chargebacks at its discretion.
dealer_actions: Contact the Hyundai Customer Care Center at 1-800-633-5151 prior to performing such a warrantable repair.
eligibility_conditions: New Vehicle Limited, Powertrain Limited, and Anti-Perforation Limited warranties apply to vehicles manufactured to United States specifications.
New Vehicle Limited, Powertrain Limited, and Anti-Perforation Limited warranties apply to vehicles distributed by Hyundai Motor America.
New Vehicle Limited, Powertrain Limited, and Anti-Perforation Limited warranties apply to vehicles registered and normally operated in the 50 United States and Washington, D.C.

Federal Emissions Defect and Emissions Performance Warranties apply to vehicles registered in the 50 United States and Washington, D.C., the Commonwealth of Puerto Rico, the Virgin Islands, Guam, and American Samoa, including the Commonwealth of the Northern Mariana Islands.
coverage_and_reimbursement: Vehicles manufactured to other than United States specifications, distributed by other than HMA, and registered and normally operated outside the 50 United States and Washington, D.C., are entitled to warranty service on the basis of the warranty applicable to such other country.
not_covered: HMA will not be liable for breach of warranty caused by or resulting from prohibition or restriction by law or regulation of any government.

HMA will not be liable for breach of warranty caused by or resulting from fire, flood, storms, earthquake, epidemic, war, strike, lockout, labor troubles, accident, riot, Acts of God, or other events beyond a party’s control.
claim_processing_risks: Hyundai Motor America reserves the right to furnish final decisions in all warranty matters.
A warranty claim chargeback may be issued at HMA’s discretion in certain circumstances.

title: What is Covered
raw_content: This section states that Hyundai covers repair or replacement of components originally manufactured or installed by Hyundai Motor Company, Hyundai Motor Group, Hyundai Motor Manufacturing Alabama, Kia Motors Manufacturing Georgia, Kia Motors Mexico, or Hyundai Motor America that are defective in material or factory workmanship under normal use and maintenance. It also covers towing to the nearest Hyundai dealership or authorized service location when the vehicle is inoperable due to a warrantable defect, and states that repairs will be made using new Hyundai Genuine Parts or Hyundai-authorized remanufactured parts.
eligibility_conditions: Component was originally manufactured or installed by Hyundai Motor Company, Hyundai Motor Group, Hyundai Motor Manufacturing Alabama, Kia Motors Manufacturing Georgia, Kia Motors Mexico, or Hyundai Motor America.
Component is found to be defective in material or factory workmanship.
Vehicle is under normal use and maintenance.
Vehicle is inoperable due to a warrantable defect for towing coverage.
coverage_and_reimbursement: Repair or replacement of covered components that are defective in material or factory workmanship.
Towing expense to the nearest Hyundai dealership or authorized service location when the vehicle is inoperable due to a warrantable defect.
Repairs will be made using new Hyundai Genuine Parts or Hyundai-authorized remanufactured parts.
not_covered: Any item specifically referred to in Section 4.1.15 What is Not Covered.
payment_limitations: Towing expense is covered within 60 months from the date of original retail delivery or date of first use, or 60,000 miles.
